CAR's Field of Dreams in NJ

Paul D'Ambrosio Asbury Park Press December 1994
Business & Economy

In the last two years, we developed a computer-assisted reporting program that is accessible to everyone at the Asbury Park Press. We provide open access to NEXIS, CompuServe, Internet, and America On...

In the last two years, we developed a computer-assisted reporting program that is accessible to everyone at the Asbury Park Press. We provide open access to NEXIS, CompuServe, Internet, and America On-line, facilitating staff-wide learning at their own pace. We've installed networked computers in bureaus and paired with software like FoxPro and SPSS. Our stories regarding local issues include exposing delinquent property taxpayers and the discrepancy in state's crime rate statistics despite legislative efforts.

A notable investigation involved analyzing tax lists which led to a named and shamed campaign on delinquent taxpayers including influential figures. A challenge remains in overcoming a reluctance to engage deeply in CAR tools among some staffers, but efforts are underway to encourage its usage.
